title: [P1] Make per-step token and cost attribution a first-class observability surface
acceptance_criteria: Every new provider request can produce exactly one canonical usage record or one explicit unavailable record.
Usage records are durably keyed to workflow, run, logical step, step execution, and attempt/turn where applicable.
Direct providers, direct Codex compatibility, and Omnigent bridge sessions use the same attribution contract.
Provider-reported, bridge-reported, estimated, and unavailable values are visibly distinct.
Pricing source/version is preserved for every estimate and historical estimates are not silently repriced.
Activity retry, stream replay, bridge reconnect, and duplicate terminal events cannot double count usage.
Failed requests with reported usage contribute to totals; unknown usage never appears as zero.
Workflow Detail shows workflow, step, and attempt/turn breakdowns with retry/remediation contribution.
Incident reconstruction and Workflow Detail resolve the same durable attribution data.
Aggregate metrics use only approved bounded labels and reconcile within documented tolerance to durable totals.
Authorization, redaction, retention, and invoice-disclaimer behavior are tested.
Coverage metrics identify runtime/provider paths that still cannot report usage.
source_resolution: Full issue body recovered through authenticated GitHub issue tooling after trusted workflow context truncation.
